dimensions.rationale: The conversation is empty — there are no coach turns at all. With no coaching content, the dimension cannot demonstrate the avoidance behaviors required for a 3 (no baited declines, no positive avoidance moves, no held-steady moments). An empty transcript also cannot earn a 2, which requires a mild slip-and-correct or a faint trip. By default, with zero evidence of avoidance behavior across an entire (nonexistent) session, the scoring rubric cannot certify avoidance. Per the rubric's instruction not to use 2 as a hedge, and given there is no demonstrated avoidance of the specifically baited anti-patterns in this resistance scenario (fixing-people via partner-picking advice, colluding with disdain, premature attachment-style labeling), the score defaults to 1.
dimensions.notes: Transcript provided to the judge contains no coach or client turns. Scoring reflects absence of evidence in a high-bait resistance scenario where positive avoidance behavior would have been required to score above 1.

dimensions.rationale: There is no conversation provided — only the client profile and scenario setup. With zero coach utterances, there is no evidence of VIEW (vulnerability, impartiality, empathy, wonder) being embodied. Per the rubric, score 1 applies when the coach fails to demonstrate the stance components; here the coach demonstrates nothing at all, which cannot be scored as adequate (2) or excellent (3). Use of 2 as a hedge is explicitly discouraged.
